text:
Electric Castle — complete guide for participants
Electric Castle is one of the largest and most immersive festivals in Romania, held at Bánffy Park in Bonțida, about 30 km from Cluj-Napoca. The edition in 2026 takes place between July 16 and 19. Officially described as a 4-day, 24/7 experience with over 250 artists, new media installations, cultural activations, and one of Europe's most appreciated camping zones.

Electric Castle is a music and culture festival that combines concerts, art, technology, social activities, and relaxation zones within an unique historic setting on the Bánffy estate. Organizers present it as a large event with annual attendance of over 250,000 people and a strong identity focused on musical diversity, high-quality production, and community experience.

A key aspect for participants is that Electric Castle operates more like a temporary mini-city rather than just a concert festival: there are stages, camping areas, food zones, bars, dedicated transport, information points, cashless systems, artistic activations, security services, and first aid.

The 2026 edition takes place between July 16 and 19 at Bánffy Castle in Bonțida, Cluj County. The festival opens on Thursday, July 16, at 14:00 and remains open until Monday, July 20, at 08:00, while the EC Village opens on Wednesday, July 15, at 12:00 and closes on Monday, July 20, at 10:00.

The location is approximately 30 km from Cluj-Napoca, and access can be made regularly by train, bus, car or other transport options provided by the organizers. The festival strongly recommends public transportation, especially dedicated EC trains and non-stop buses.

For 2026, Electric Castle announced over 80 artists across 10 stages with headliners like The Cure and Twenty One Pilots, plus many other international names from rock, indie, electronic, and alternative genres. Other press sources have confirmed that the complete lineup exceeds 200 acts, including artists such as Teddy Swims, Chase & Status, Nothing But Thieves, Wet Leg, Yung Lean, Bladee, Subtronics, and others.

From a participant's perspective, this means the program is very dense and you need to choose when you want to see something or risk missing good sets due to overlaps. The official app is mentioned as the primary tool for managing the schedule, map, and personalizing your agenda.

Electric Castle offers more categories of access: General Access Pass, Youth Pass U25, VIP Pass, Ultra VIP Pass, Black Ticket, Camping Pass, and daily tickets for each day.

In 2026, on the ticket page, options with insurance will appear alongside single-day tickets and premium one-day tickets.

In short:

The General Access Pass grants access to the entire festival.

Youth Pass U25 is for participants under 25 years old.

VIP Pass and Ultra VIP offer access to premium zones and additional experiences.

A Camping Pass is required if you want to camp.

Daily Ticket provides access for a single day.

The Black Ticket offers the most exclusive access, including extended access to premium areas and backstage, according to the official FAQ.

On the 2026 ticket page, prices are displayed with General Access Pass at €250, Youth Pass U25 at €139, VIP Pass at €350, Ultra VIP at €699, Black Ticket at €4999, and Camping Pass at €69. Day-of tickets cost €119 each, while Any Day Premium Tickets are listed at €249.

At the venue, there are various accommodation add-ons available, ranging from pre-pitched tents to cabins and lodges with different prices depending on capacity and comfort. Additionally, for camping car and RV, there are dedicated options available, which are explicitly mentioned in the ticket page as Car Camping and RV Camping.

Tickets can be purchased on the official website or through the Electric Castle app. After purchase, tickets appear in your account under My Orders from where they can be downloaded as a PDF and presented at entry or printed.

An important aspect is that tickets are non-refundable: there must be names and address of the ticket holder identical to their ID present on the ticket, plus an uploaded photo in EC's account. The organizers state that personalization is free for the first 7 days after purchase, with a personalization fee applying thereafter.

If you have a voucher or invitation, it must be redeemed before the specified date; vouchers alone do not allow entry to the festival. Tickets can also be exchanged through the official exchange platform, but only for tickets purchased officially.

You need a valid, personalized ticket and proper identification for entry. At check-in, your identity is verified against the data on your ticket, and without this you won't get your wristband and can't enter.

Organizers recommend pre-swapping of the wristband at EC Village locations in Cluj and Bucharest to avoid lines upon entry. Pre-swapping is not available for day tickets, and minors are only allowed to swap their wristbands at the festival.

Electric Castle uses a cashless RFID wristband system, which becomes your wallet for food, drinks, and merchandise. You can top up online in advance of the festival or on-site at top-up points, and the wristband must be activated during the festival to use the charged funds.

Payments at the festival are, in practice, cashless; the FAQ page states that the official currency used is Romanian leu, but payments are made via the wristband or card, at some vendors and bars. On-site top-ups have limits, and there are procedures for refunds at the end of the event, with different fees and timeframes depending on the method used.

There are multiple transportation options, but official ones are encouraged to be the trains dedicated and the non-stop buses. The festival is located 30 km from Cluj-Napoca, and transportation is built around the roads connecting Cluj-Bonțida and the return route.
section_title: Cum ajungi la festival — Prezentare generală

text: Electric Castle trains depart from Cluj-Napoca's Small Railway Station and travel to Jucu, where they continue by bus to the festival site. The EC train ticket is presented in the FAQ as being 35 lei and covering the entire journey from Cluj-Bontida.
section_title: Cum ajungi la festival — Trenuri EC

text: Non-stop buses depart from the area around Iulius Mall and Expo Transylvania day and night. The bus ticket is still 35 lei per direction, and organizers recommend purchasing in advance.

You can come by regular CFR train to Bonțida station and then walk approximately 40 minutes down to the entrance. This is a less convenient option than the EC train or bus, but viable if you cannot afford dedicated transportation tickets.

Organizers strongly discourage income from car rentals, but if you choose this option, you must use the parking and respect the designated traffic access. For cars, there are special rules, dedicated parking areas, and in some cases, separate tickets for Car Camping or RV Camping.
section_title: Cum ajungi la festival — Cu mașina

text: For those coming from outside the city, Cluj Airport is recommended. From there, you can continue with EC transport, a taxi, or ridesharing. Official information also mentions special access for Uber and taxis, including designated pick-up/drop-off points and driver rules.
section_title: Cum ajungi la festival — Taxi, Uber, aeroport

text:
The official camping area for Electric Castle (EC Village) is just a 5-7 minute walk downhill from the festival entrance and is designed as an entirely separate space rather than just another section of the grounds. There you'll find premium bathrooms and showers, a beauty area, market stalls, grill zones, breakfast and coffee lounges, yoga and massage spots, a music stage, campfires, and customer service points.

The camping area is open 24 hours a day throughout the festival period, and to enter requires a Camping Pass. If you want to stay in glamping structures, you'll also need the appropriate ticket for that type of accommodation, plus both the Festival Pass and the Camping Pass as per EC Village's page.

In the camping area, items such as luggage, food, water in appropriate containers, hygiene products, original medicine packaging, professional photo/video equipment for personal use, frames, stands, catering and foldable furniture are permitted. The organizers also allow small folding chairs or meals that can be stored inside when leaving the camping area.
section_title: Camping: EC Village — Ce poți aduce în camping

text: Alcohol, illegal substances, flammable materials, generators, electrical appliances, refrigerators, umbrellas, pavilion awnings, large fans, weapons, knives, glass, animals, bicycles, and drones are prohibited. The list is designed for safety, comfort, and avoiding incidents.
section_title: Camping: EC Village — Ce nu poți aduce în camping

text:
In addition to its own tents, Electric Castle offers more glamping and structured accommodation options. On the official website listing, there are various types of accommodations such as Pre-Pitched Tent, Tall Nest, Hygge Tent, Fancy Cabin, Wooden Cabin, Dream Tent, The Boss, Blue Chalet, Panorama Tent, Swedish Lodge, White Lodge, Car Camping, and RV Camping.

The cabins differ in terms of the number of people they accommodate and level of comfort: some are for 2 people, others for 4, 5 or 6, while some have enhanced capacity or better amenities than traditional camping. For a participant, this means you can choose between very basic experiences and almost hotel-like experiences within the festival grounds.

The festival has a diverse food zones offering raw/vegan, Indian, Mexican, Italian, Romanian, burger options, desserts, salads, and fine dining. The organizers explicitly mention that there are also various beverages ranging from water and juice to wine, long drinks, and shots.

There are free refillable water points, info points, first aid stations, recovery zones, magazines, phone charging lockers with mobile app maps, schedules, and alerts. There are also cultural activity zones, sports areas, fashion markets, and eco-spaces as per the official communications and press reports.

Electric Castle ensures safety with a Red Team, a safety line, first aid points, and discreet support systems for sensitive situations. On their official website, organizers provide the "Angel Shot" code at bars for individuals needing protection or support without openly communicating with others nearby.

There are also dedicated phone lines for support, plus a WhatsApp channel for transportation, mentioned in promotional materials and public guides for the festival. Additionally, there are recommendations for protecting personal belongings and using lockers.

You are not allowed to bring alcohol to the festival or camping area, nor any glass, knives, weapons, illegal substances, drones, bicycles in the festival area, or other dangerous objects. In the festival area, shadows are prohibited, and there are additional restrictions for generators, cooking appliances, or large electrical equipment at camping.

You can take photos and videos for personal use only, but you are not allowed to distribute material for commercial or promotional purposes. Additionally, filming and photography may be restricted entirely during some concerts, which the organizers announce through apps and social media.

Children aged up to 7 years old can enter for free if accompanied by an adult with a valid ticket. Minors under the age of 16 need parental or legal guardian consent and must be accompanied by a responsible adult; one adult can accompany up to three minors.

Young adults between 16-17 require parental permission to participate. The FAQ also mentions the recommendation for using quiet wristbands for very young children, especially those under 4 years old.

The official FAQ states that persons with disabilities have free entry, and the festival area is wheelchair accessible. Separate toilets are available, and there are ramps connecting the stage to barriers at each stage, making access more user-friendly than in many traditional festivals.

Electric Castle is built as a 24/7 festival, so the experience doesn't just revolve around daytime concerts. Daytime zones include exploration areas, food stalls, lounges, activities, and art. The nighttime features stages, dancing, and illuminated spaces all around the castle.

For a participant, the key is to treat the festival as a 4-day mini-vacation: you need a sleep plan, a phone charging plan, transportation plans, and a cashless system budget in lei. If staying at a campsite, comfort matters greatly: bedding, sleeping bag, clothes for nighttime cold, and waterproof protection.

Before the festival is it good to have:

The correct personalized ticket, with name, address and photo.

A valid ID card.

A backpack and a cashless account created in advance.

The transportation reserved, especially if you want to take the EC train or bus.

The campsite or camping purchased in advance, as good options tend to fill up quickly.

If you are going to the camping, prepare for changing weather: it can be hot during the day and cool at night, and sometimes you might need a raincoat, sleeping bag, and layered clothing. The guides and participant discussions show that these details make a big difference between having a good experience or an unpleasant one.

A useful list for a participant should include:

Identity document.

Customized personal ticket and possibly printed.

Mobile phone + charger + power bank.

Day clothes and warmer clothing for night.

Comfortable, waterproof outerwear.

Raincoat.

Sunscreen and lip balm or chapstick.

Plastic bottle or container allowed for water.

Personal medications in their original packaging.

Camping gear if staying at EC Village.

Sleeping bag, pillow sack, and sleeping mat if you don't have glamping.

The Electric Castle food area is one of the festival's strong points. There are plenty of options and it includes choices for almost any dietary preference, with beverages sold only through a cashless system in reusable containers, with a guarantee on cups.

Organizers also use a reusable cup system, with an initial deposit of 10 lei that can be returned at the end. This means less plastic waste, less littering, and better logistics in crowded areas.

Electric Castle is not an inexpensive festival, especially if you add the main ticket, camping, transportation, accommodation, meal plans, and top-up for consumption. In addition to the price of the ticket, there's a booking fee of 8% on most options, and premium or glamping services can significantly increase the total cost.

In practice, your budget will depend on your festival style: if you camp in simple conditions and eat packed lunches, you can control costs; if you choose VIP, glamping, and large consumption, the total cost increases quickly. It's important to note that a refund from camping may be subject to processing fees under certain circumstances.

For your first visit to Electric Castle, it works best if:

Arrive a day or a night in advance and do pre-swapping.

Reserve your transport in advance.

Use the official app for the schedule.

Bring water and keep your top-up under control.

Plan some breaks during the day.

Don't underestimate nighttime cold and accumulated fatigue.

If you're bringing fuel, a simple plan is to establish beforehand a meeting point, a shared budget, and a list of priority artists. At a large festival without some basic organization, you'll waste time wandering around, in your tent, or overlapping schedules.

Electric Castle is suitable for people who want more than just a large concert: varied music, a community atmosphere, camping, visual arts, good food, and a four-day experience in an historic location. If you prefer maximum comfort and simple logistics, premium tickets or glamping can help, but even the standard variant is thoughtfully designed for a big festival.

If you only want to see one artist and leave, a day ticket will suffice, but you'll miss much of the festival's identity. In fact, EC was built more for those who stay at the festival, move between stages, and use the camping as part of their experience.
